evals.prompt: find papers about AI for requirements engineering, only CCF-A and CCF-B, paper length >= 8
evals.expected_output: A list of relevant CCF-A and CCF-B papers on AI for RE, with their BibTeX entries saved to a .bib file in the current directory.
expectations: The output includes at least 1 CCF-B SE paper (RE conference papers on AI/LLM for RE)
A .bib file is created in the current directory
The BibTeX entries contain DOI, author, title, booktitle, year, and pages fields
Papers are from CCF-A or CCF-B venues only (ICSE, RE, etc.)

evals.prompt: generate a literature review on LLM for requirements engineering, CCF-B SE, 2024-2025. Save output to /tmp/llm-re-review/
evals.expected_output: A .md literature review and .bib file saved to /tmp/llm-re-review/
expectations: Two files are created: a .md report and a .bib file in /tmp/llm-re-review/
The report groups papers by CCF rank (CCF-A SE, CCF-B SE sections)
Each paper has a DOI link in the report
The BibTeX uses CrossRef source (verified by checking for 'RE63999' or 'RE59067' DOI prefixes in entries)
At least 2 papers are found from RE 2024-2025

evals.prompt: fetch BibTeX for: conf/re/BarzaminiR22 conf/re/FengMYSBAMTBCCC24 conf/re/GuardadoPZRT25. Save to /tmp/fetch-test.bib
evals.expected_output: A BibTeX file at /tmp/fetch-test.bib with 3 entries
expectations: The file contains at least 2 BibTeX entries (some may fail if rate-limited)
Each entry has author, title, booktitle, year, and doi fields
The script uses CrossRef (DOI-based fetch) for at least one entry
